notes: Central focus of study; combines RDBMS with multidimensional query capability; multi-tier architecture
Proprietary MDB-based OLAP; scalability limited to 20-50GB; faces competition from ROLAP
Specialized storage using array/sparse-matrix technology; becoming impractical above 50GB
Performance and storage optimization that hunts unused MDB cells and compresses arrays; used by Pilot and Express
RDBMSs scaling beyond 100GB; exemplified by Informix-Online; foundation for ROLAP
Organizes data into fact tables (measures) and dimension tables (satellite tables); core to ROLAP
Standard interface used by report-writer tools to connect to databases; enables end-user query building
Core query mechanism; Relational OLAP generates multi-pass SQL dynamically; layperson-baffling per study
Used to translate technical schema to business terms; critical for ROLAP change management
Repetitively stores data to minimize joins; key ROLAP database optimization technique
Pre-aggregating data to eliminate runtime calculation; one of three core ROLAP optimization techniques
Divides large fact tables into smaller tables; improves query response time and warehouse maintenance
MicroStrategy OLAP engine capability; generates complex multi-pass SQL to avoid need for specialized MDB
Entire application resides on desktop; weakness exposed when querying large datasets; being superseded
Central enterprise RDBMS optimized for decision support; scaling to terabytes by 1995
Server-based MDB; later became Oracle Essbase after Arbor-Hyperion-Oracle acquisition chain
Server-based MDB listed alongside Essbase and Express; acquired into Seagate/Crystal/Business Objects
Server-based MDB with sparse-matrix; Oracle acquired IRI Software for this product in 1995
Client-side MDB maintaining pre-calculated data in PC memory; handles a few megabytes
High-end report writer; IS pre-defines complex SQL (Group By, Having, Union) for business users
High-end report writer alongside BusinessObjects; requires IS to predefined SQL and maintain metadata
Predefined query tool cited as report-writer/predefined category example; now part of SAP
Low-end personal database tool; cited as encouraging incorrect query results via joined intermediary tables
Spreadsheet allowing small dataset cross-tab views; lowest tier of multidimensional tools
Low-end personal database tool alongside Microsoft Access; Lotus acquired by IBM in 1995
Monitors query environment; runs background analyses; notifies workers of predefined business conditions
Emerging application class enabled by ROLAP; allows drilling for information in any direction without pre-programmed paths
Databases exceeding 100GB; hundreds of sites by 1995; ROLAP leverages RDBMS VLDB support
